name: Black-Eyed Pea-Corn Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp Peanut oil (available at Asian markets)
1 cup Scallions; chopped, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small nonstick skillet, heat sesame and peanut oil over medium heat. Add scallions and ginger. Cook, stirring frequently for 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
2. Whisk together orange juice, rice wine vinegar, soy sauce, granulated sugar, and chili paste in a bowl until well combined. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine cooked corn, diced red and yellow bell peppers, chopped chicory, and drained black-eyed peas. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to coat evenly.
4. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Each serving provides: 1 Fat-Adjustment (FA), 1 Protein, 2 Vitamins (V), 1/2 Beta-Carotene (B), 5 Carbohydrates (C). Per serving: 186 calories, 7 grams protein, 5 grams fat, 31 grams carbohydrates, 418 milligrams sodium, 0 grams cholesterol.
